checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2013-03-31 value=47500419.50 vs end=2012-12-31 value=35928550.94 (+32%)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill end=2011-12-31 value=653673811.86 growing to end=2012-12-31 value=745766616.73 (+14.1%)
Assets end=2012-09-30 value=854982263.71 jumping to end=2012-12-31 value=1045938956.97 (+22.3% in one quarter)
LongTermDebtNoncurrent end=2011-12-31 value=131057933.47 growing to end=2013-09-30 value=579182621.11 (~4.4x in ~21 months)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet or CostOfGoodsSold line items provided in the dataset

OperatingIncomeLoss/Revenues margin roughly stable: OperatingIncomeLoss 2012 value=90802312.04 / Revenues 2012 value=149118089.77 (~61%) vs 2011 OperatingIncomeLoss=96414396.46/Revenues=155872736.58 (~62%)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Liabilities end=2011-12-31 value=365707207.33 growing to end=2013-09-30 value=739875529.62
DeferredRevenueCurrent end=2012-12-31 value=3816267.98 growing to end=2013-09-30 value=17954792.75
No allowance for doubtful accounts or specific reserve line item provided in dataset

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Elevated 8-K frequency in 2012-2013 including multiple 8-K/A amendments (2013-05-13, 2013-06-28) coinciding with large acquisitions/debt issuances

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided continuously from 2008-01-01 through 2013-09-30 across Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, Assets, Liabilities, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, and cash flow statement line items

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition / extended customer credit terms causing receivables to outpace revenue growth, especially as revenue declined in FY2012 (Revenues=149118089.77) even as receivables grew (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2012-12-31=35928550.94) and then accelerated sharply into Q1 2013 (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2013-03-31=47500419.50, +32% q/q vs revenue +23% q/q)

rationale_evidence.quote: Revenues 2012 value=149118089.77 vs 2011 value=155872736.58 (revenue declined -4.3%) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 33576836.38 (2011-12-31) to 35928550.94 (2012-12-31) (+7%)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Aggressive/optimistic purchase price allocation in debt-funded acquisitions inflating recorded intangible asset and goodwill values relative to underlying organic revenue generation, with total assets nearly doubling (Assets end=2011-12-31=910890273.48 to end=2013-09-30=1201842459.71) funded largely by a >4x increase in long-term debt (LongTermDebtNoncurrent end=2011-12-31=131057933.47 to end=2013-09-30=579182621.11), while revenue growth was flat-to-negative in the same window (Revenues 2012=149118089.77 vs 2011=155872736.58) and stockholders' equity declined despite asset growth (StockholdersEquity end=2012-12-31=499908346.39 to end=2013-09-30=407571098.07)

rationale_evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill end=2012-09-30 value=650038347.75 jumping to end=2012-12-31 value=745766616.73 (+14.7% in one quarter)
StockholdersEquity end=2012-12-31 value=499908346.39 declining to end=2013-09-30 value=407571098.07 despite Assets rising from 1045938956.97 to 1201842459.71

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Recurring annual 10-K/A amendments (2011, 2012, 2013) and a 10-Q/A amendment in 2011 suggest either routine Part III supplementation or possible correction of previously filed disclosures; combined with unusually heavy 8-K/8-K-A activity during the 2012-2013 acquisition wave, this pattern warrants scrutiny for delayed or corrected financial disclosures tied to the debt-funded acquisition accounting noted above

rationale_evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2011-04-29, 2012-04-27, and 2013-04-30, each following the original 10-K by roughly two months
10-Q/A filed 2011-08-30 amending the 10-Q originally filed 2011-08-09/2011-08-30 window

top_signals: Accounts receivable growing materially faster than revenue in 2012 (AR +7% vs revenue -4.3%) and accelerating further into Q1 2013 (AR +32% q/q vs revenue +23% q/q)

Large debt-funded acquisitions ballooning intangible assets and total assets (Assets nearly doubled 2011->2013, LongTermDebtNoncurrent grew ~4.4x) while organic revenue growth was flat-to-negative in 2012, raising purchase-accounting/impairment risk

Recurring annual 10-K/A amendments (2011, 2012, 2013) plus a 10-Q/A, alongside heightened 8-K/8-K-A frequency during the acquisition period, indicating potential disclosure or control process irregularities
Stockholders' equity declined in 2013 even as total assets grew sharply, consistent with heavy debt-financed growth diluting balance sheet quality
